title: rubus × infructuosus
intro: Rubus × infructuosus, a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Rubus idaeus (raspberry) and Rubus fruticosus (blackberry), is a type of bramble that is primarily found in Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to its high content of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Rubus × infructuosus is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the digestive system. Rubus × infructuosus is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or mixing them with other herbs.
